name: Automated Backup Script with Rotation
description: Create a bash script that performs timestamped backups of files and directories with configurable retention and automatic rotation of old backups.

steps.title: Create the backup script
steps.description: Write a script that creates timestamped compressed backups of a source directory.
steps.command:
cat > backup.sh << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SOURCE_DIR="${1:?'Usage: backup.sh <source> <dest> [retention_days]'}"
BACKUP_DIR="${2:?'Usage: backup.sh <source> <dest> [retention_days]'}"
RETENTION_DAYS="${3:-30}"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
BACKUP_NAME="backup_${TIMESTAMP}.tar.gz"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
tar -czf "$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME" -C "$(dirname "$SOURCE_DIR")" "$(basename "$SOURCE_DIR")"
echo "Backup created: $BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME"
EOF
chmod +x backup.sh

steps.title: Add backup rotation
steps.description: Delete backups older than the retention period to prevent disk space exhaustion.
steps.command:
cat >> backup.sh << 'EOF'

# Rotate old backups
DELETED=$(find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name 'backup_*.tar.gz' -mtime +"$RETENTION_DAYS" -print -delete | wc -l)
echo "Rotated $DELETED backups older than $RETENTION_DAYS days"
EOF
steps.warning: Always test the find -delete command with -print first to verify which files would be removed before adding -delete.

steps.title: Add backup verification
steps.description: Verify the backup archive is valid and not corrupted after creation.
steps.command: echo 'tar -tzf "$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME" > /dev/null && echo "Backup verified successfully" || { echo "Backup verification FAILED" >&2; exit 1; }' >> backup.sh

steps.title: Schedule with cron
steps.description: Add a cron job to run the backup daily at 2 AM.
steps.tip: Use >> to append to the log file and 2>&1 to capture both stdout and stderr.

fullScript:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Automated Backup with Rotation
SOURCE_DIR="${1:?'Usage: backup.sh <source> <dest> [retention_days]'}"
BACKUP_DIR="${2:?'Usage: backup.sh <source> <dest> [retention_days]'}"
RETENTION_DAYS="${3:-30}"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
BACKUP_NAME="backup_${TIMESTAMP}.tar.gz"

log() { echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*"; }

# Validate source
[[ ! -d "$SOURCE_DIR" ]] && { echo "Source not found: $SOURCE_DIR" >&2; exit 1; }

# Create backup directory
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Create backup
log "Backing up $SOURCE_DIR -> $BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME"
tar -czf "$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME" -C "$(dirname "$SOURCE_DIR")" "$(basename "$SOURCE_DIR")"

# Get size
SIZE=$(du -h "$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME" | cut -f1)
log "Backup size: $SIZE"

# Verify
tar -tzf "$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME" > /dev/null
log "Backup verified successfully"

# Rotate old backups
DELETED=$(find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name 'backup_*.tar.gz' -mtime +"$RETENTION_DAYS" -print -delete | wc -l)
log "Rotated $DELETED old backups (retention: $RETENTION_DAYS days)"

# Summary
TOTAL=$(find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name 'backup_*.tar.gz' | wc -l)
log "Total backups: $TOTAL"

faq.question: How do I backup a database instead of files?
faq.answer: Replace the tar command with a database dump: pg_dump dbname > backup.sql for PostgreSQL, mysqldump dbname > backup.sql for MySQL, or mongodump --out backup/ for MongoDB. Then compress the dump file.

faq.question: Should I use rsync instead of tar?
faq.answer: Use tar for full compressed backups with rotation. Use rsync for incremental syncing where only changed files are transferred: rsync -avz --delete source/ backup/. rsync is better for large datasets with small daily changes.

faq.question: How do I send backup notifications on failure?
faq.answer: Add a trap that sends an alert on error: trap 'curl -X POST -d "Backup failed on $(hostname)" https://hooks.slack.com/your-webhook' ERR. Or use mail: trap 'echo "Backup failed" | mail -s "Alert" admin@example.com' ERR.
